definition: Hiding implementation details behind a well-defined interface so higher-level code can reason about "what" without "how"
A function that either takes another function as an argument or returns a function as its result
A function bundled together with the lexical environment it was defined in
column3: capturing free variables from that scope

definition: A function call whose result is returned directly by the caller — can be optimized to reuse the caller's stack frame
A minimal formal system based on function abstraction and application; the foundation of functional programming
A property where an expression can always be replaced by its value without changing program behavior
A model of computation where evaluation happens within frames of variable bindings
Lazily-evaluated infinite sequences constructed with delayed pairs; enable representing conceptually-infinite data
